{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "tested/inspected",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective"],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 5006,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 10.0,
  "rate": 459.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Standing decay test rose from 416 microns to 5006 microns in 10 minutes after pump isolation",
    "Vacuum decay rate of ~459 microns/minute indicates significant leak or outgassing",
    "System fails to hold manufacturer-specified deep vacuum"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Re-evacuate system and perform leak detection on all brazed joints, valves, and service ports",
    "Repair identified leaks, replace damaged cores or gaskets, and re-test decay",
    "Do not charge system until decay test passes per OEM specification"
  ],
  "rationale": "A properly evacuated and sealed refrigeration system should hold a deep vacuum with minimal rise during a standing decay test. A rise from 416 to 5006 microns in 10 minutes is excessive and indicates a leak or significant contamination/outgassing. This fails standard HVAC-R evacuation acceptance criteria and manufacturer specifications.",
  "references": ["EPA 608", "mfr specs"]
}
